description: This figure presents load-deflection curves from Charpy impact tests for two low-alloy ultrahigh strength steels, i.e., 0Al steel (without high-density NiAl clusters) and 1.3Al steel (with high-density coherent NiAl clusters). Subplot (a) compares the two load-deflection curves, marking yield load (P_GY, square symbol) and maximum load (P_M, circle symbol) for each sample. Subplots (b) and (c) show the integrated load-energy-deflection curves for 0Al and 1.3Al steel, respectively. The 0Al steel has a peak load P_M of 39.7 kN, crack initiation energy E_i of 45.0 J, crack propagation energy E_p of 17.4 J, and total impact energy KU₂ of 62.4 J, with peak load reached at 2.06 mm deflection. The 1.3Al steel shows a peak load P_M of 38.6 kN, E_i of 62.0 J (37.8% higher than 0Al), E_p of 21.0 J (20.7% higher than 0Al), total KU₂ of 83.0 J, with peak load achieved at 2.55 mm deflection. Both steels show rapid load drop after peak load without obvious crack arrest stage. The results demonstrate that the introduction of high-density NiAl clusters significantly improves both crack initiation and propagation resistance, leading to superior impact toughness of the 1.3Al steel, supporting the core conclusion that NiAl clusters enable excellent strength-toughness synergy in this steel system.
